content:
Hi all, I was looking at the issue #1022 a while ago and @Miguel Ojeda point me to the ML RFC (https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20230814092302.1903203-1-dallerivemichele@gmail.com/)

I would like to ask what the status is here because I would like to help with this part of the kernel if there are needs of help

Hi Vincenzo - In addition to what Miguel said above, the (as far as I know) latest version of the patch set is also available here https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/pull/1066 . There was some upcoming development in the standard library (parts of the network related code being moved to core) that potentially impacted this patch set, @Trevor Gross can probably say more about that. If I recall correctly those would require some changes to the patches, maybe get in touch with @Michele Dalle Rive to see what is the status on that.
Perhaps @Michele Dalle Rive knows some more, but to my knowledge that PR is the latest. I was just talking about core::net which exists now, so we don't have to recreate those types on our end

Sorry if I have all this dealy I was taking some time to look inside the patch set.

Looks like that these patch are already included in core::net, so if there is anything to do around these area keep me in mind anyway :)

Vincenzo Palazzo said:

Looks like that these patch are already included in core::net, so if there is anything to do around these area keep me in mind anyway :)

I think the socket abstractions are still looking for an owner. As far as I recalled @Michele Dalle Rive did not push them further due to lack of a user. They are used in the rust nbd driver, but that one is not currently being rebased.

Yes, my original patches had no in-tree user, so they were "rejected". As @Valentin Obst pointed out, there's a PR into rust-net with the abstractions, rebased and improved, also considering core::net; in a few weeks, I'll finally have some time to work on the pr

Andreas Hindborg said:

urther due to lack of a user. They are used

Uh thanks! well maybe we can push the work forward in a join work with @Michele Dalle Rive
